wisdomEntries.insight: Open a Scotiabank StartRight account the first week. They have a specific newcomer program with no fees for one year and they don't require credit history. Don't waste time at TD or RBC first — they'll turn you away without a SIN.

Apply for a secured credit card immediately after your bank account. Put $500 on it, use it for groceries only, pay it off every month. In 6 months you'll have enough history for a real card. This is the single most important financial move you make in year one.

The OHIP 3-month wait is real. Buy Manulife CoverMe visitor insurance for exactly 90 days the day you land. It costs around $120 and covers emergencies. Don't skip this — one ER visit without it costs $3,000+.

Your Egyptian degree and experience are invisible here. Register with ACCES Employment within your first month — they have a specific tech bridging program and will connect you with Canadian employers who are open to newcomers. Got my first Canadian job through them in 4 months.

Don't only hang out with other Egyptians — I made this mistake for 8 months. Join a recreational sports league or a professional meetup in your field. Your Canadian network is what gets you your second job.

Start the CNO registration process before you land if possible. The credential assessment alone takes 3-4 months. I wasted 6 months thinking I could start after arrival. Contact CNO the day you get your work permit and begin immediately.

RBC Newcomer Advantage was best for us — they have Punjabi-speaking advisors at the Mississauga branches and understood our situation. Bring your passport, landing papers, and proof of address. They opened our account same day.

Register your kids for school immediately. The TDSB has a Welcome Centre specifically for newcomer children — they assess their level and place them appropriately. Don't wait for September — they can start mid-year.

Apply for Canada Child Benefit the week you land — not after your first tax return. You can apply with your landing documents. We missed 4 months of payments because nobody told us this. That was $1,400 we didn't get.

The Mississauga South Asian community is huge but insular. The best thing we did was join a mixed newcomer group through the Mississauga Library settlement services. Met our closest Canadian friends there — people from 8 different countries all figuring it out together.

Do not sign a lease before you arrive. Stay in a furnished short-term rental for your first month and learn the neighbourhoods. I signed a lease in Surrey sight unseen and spent a year regretting it. The $400 extra per month for a short-term place is worth every cent.

Engineers Canada and EGBC (BC) are separate bodies. You need both assessed. Start with Engineers Canada for the academic assessment, then EGBC for the provincial license. Budget 12-18 months and around $800 in fees. There is no shortcut.

LinkedIn is everything in Vancouver tech and engineering. Update your profile the week you land, connect with every Canadian engineer you meet, and post about your arrival and job search. I got 3 interviews from a single post about my experience.

Nobody talks about how lonely the first 6 months are. I was successful in Colombia — manager of 40 people — and in Canada I felt invisible. This is normal. Find a therapist through BetterHelp or the Vancouver Newcomer Health Centre. Talking to someone saved my year.

File your taxes even if you arrived in November and only worked 2 weeks. You are entitled to a partial year GST/HST credit and potentially a BC climate action tax credit. H&R Block has a newcomer tax service for $80 that is worth it for your first return.
